story.text: As the loop, I need to prove the L0 value gate can actually say no — not trust a 3.4% refusal rate as if it were calibration. The gate's own prompt names the debt ('semantic evals are owed'). bdo's frame is charades: give each adversarial case three turns (variants escalating toward the fluent-but-wrong edge) and judge each by the reaction of the whole room (a panel of independent judges, not a solo verdict). A gate that bites only obvious junk but folds to a dressed-up story is brittle; a gate that refuses everything is paranoid; both are as broken as a rubber stamp. This is the eval that tells them apart, with teeth that fail all three.

briefing.headline: the value-gate eval: does the L0 gate actually bite?
briefing.value: loop/gate_eval.py — a stdlib, read-only harness + a committed adversarial corpus (loop/gate_eval.cases.json) where each case is a chef with a 3-turn matched-variant axis (plain -> dressed-up -> fluent-but-wrong edge), plus a genuine accept control. Each variant is judged by a panel ('the room') of independent dry judges; the scorer folds the room's reception (majority + spread) and computes catch-rate, bucket-match, and a robustness curve, with a pass/fail that fails a rubber-stamp gate, a paranoid gate, AND a brittle gate.
briefing.why_now: bdo's direction this session: an /insights crawl showed the gates refuse ~3.4% of the time; by §10 that is no proof the second-set-of-eyes can say no. He chose 'make the gate bite' and named the charades method (3 turns, judged by the whole room).
briefing.if_accepted: The loop gains a reusable, toothed instrument for whether any gate's prompt actually discriminates. The first live run is recorded: value-gate.claude.v1 bit the no-value case on all three turns INCLUDING the dressed-up surface_trap (unanimous reject_no_value across a panel of 3 per turn) and accepted the genuine control (unanimous) — catch-rate 1.00 over the judged should-reject turns, coverage 2/4 cases. The 3.4% historical rate is not a toothless gate; it is a gate fed only announce-late good work.
briefing.if_rejected: The gates' refusal rate stays an unexamined number — we never learn whether a low rate means good work or a soft gate, and we cannot tell a brittle gate (fooled by a re-dress) from a robust one.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and fail-safe: read-only and deterministic; the corpus is declared eval input, never minted onto the pipeline or written through loop.node; the live judges are dry (verdict returned as text, no receipt, no log write). Reverting is deleting the module.
briefing.mechanism: loop/gate_eval.py: load_corpus / reception / score / render, CLI renders the corpus and scores a panel transcript. loop/gate_eval.cases.json is the corpus (4 cases spanning reject_no_value, reject_wrong_value, amend, accept; reject cases carry a 3-turn axis). loop/gate_eval.first-run.json records the live panel verdicts. tests/test_gate_eval.py joins the suite with §10 teeth: an all-accept transcript FAILS (rubber-stamp), an all-reject transcript FAILS the control (paranoid), a turn-1-bites/turn-3-misses transcript is flagged BRITTLE, a discriminating+robust transcript passes, and a case with empty rooms reads as not_run (NOT a soft-gate failure — the false positive the first live run exposed and this fix removes). Done-line 0116.
